airflow.name: airflow
airflow.short_description: Platform created by the community to programmatically author, schedule and monitor workflows.

features.description: Backed by the ASF's dedicated security team and sponsorship program.
features.title: Apache Software Foundation backing

features.description: Workflows are written as Python code structured into Directed Acyclic Graphs, with explicit task dependencies.

features.description: Failed tasks can be retried automatically and trigger alerts.
features.title: Automatic retries and failure alerting

features.description: Pipelines are plain Python, so Airflow can connect to virtually any data source or destination reachable from a Python library.
features.title: Python-native integrations

pros: Backed by the Apache Software Foundation's dedicated security team and sponsorship program
Large community with regular in-person meetups
cons: Steeper learning curve than drag-and-drop workflow builders, since pipelines are defined in Python code

faqs.answer: Yes — Airflow is a free, open-source Apache Software Foundation project.

archivebox.name: archivebox
archivebox.short_description: ArchiveBox is open-source self-hosted web archiving for preserving websites, bookmarks, RSS feeds, social posts, media, evidence, research sources, and institutional records in durable HTML, PDF, PNG, WARC, JSON, SQLite, and filesystem formats.

features.description: Preserves web pages on infrastructure you control.

infisical.name: Infisical
infisical.short_description: Open-source platform for managing application secrets, PKI certificates, and privileged access across cloud and AI infrastructure.

features.description: Centralized storage, rotation, and auditing of application credentials with RBAC, approval workflows, and dynamic secret issuance.
features.title: Secrets management

features.description: Automates private certificate authority operations including issuance, renewal, revocation, and discovery.
features.title: Certificate management (PKI)

features.description: Grants short-lived, identity-based access sessions with session recording and AI-generated summaries.
features.title: Privileged access management (PAM)

features.description: Gives AI agents access to credentials they need without exposing the underlying secret values directly to the agent.
features.title: Agent Vault

features.description: Issues short-lived, on-demand credentials scoped to individual requests instead of long-lived static secrets.
features.title: Dynamic secrets

features.description: Connects with AWS, Kubernetes, Docker, GitHub, GitLab, Terraform, Ansible, Jenkins, Vercel, Heroku, Azure, GCP, and Datadog.
features.title: Broad integrations

pros: Free-forever plan covering up to 5 identities and 3 projects
Open source with a self-hosting option in addition to cloud
Combines secrets management, PKI, and privileged access management in one platform
Wide range of native integrations across cloud and CI/CD tooling
cons: Pro plan is priced per identity ($18/month), which scales with team/agent count
Advanced capabilities like dynamic secrets, AI security advisor, SCIM, and LDAP require the custom-priced Enterprise plan
Free tier is capped at 5 identities, 3 projects, 3 environments, and 10 integrations

faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan is free forever and includes up to 5 identities, 3 projects, and 3 environments.

faqs.answer: Yes, through Agent Vault, which lets AI agents use credentials without exposing the secret values directly.

features: Higher rate limits and priority support

parse-platform.name: Parse Platform
parse-platform.short_description: Parse Platform (Parse Server) is a free, open-source Node.js backend providing REST/GraphQL APIs, real-time queries, push notifications, and file storage.

Supports both MongoDB and PostgreSQL (with PostGIS) as the backing database
Deployable to many platforms: Docker, Heroku, AWS Elastic Beanstalk, Google App Engine, Azure, DigitalOcean
SDKs available for major client platforms

Relies on community and Open Collective sponsorship rather than a commercial vendor for ongoing support
Requires running and maintaining your own Node.js and database infrastructure

novu.name: Novu
novu.short_description: Novu is an open-source notification infrastructure platform for multi-channel workflows across email, SMS, push, and chat.

features.description: Sends notifications across 9+ channels including email, in-app inbox, SMS, push, and chat apps like Slack and Microsoft Teams.

features.description: Consolidates multiple alerts into a single digest message.

pros: Open source with an MIT-licensed core (open-core model) and roughly 39,000 GitHub stars

Supports 9+ notification channels from one API
Self-hosted deployment available on the Enterprise plan
US and EU data residency options
cons: Paid plans start at $30/month once usage exceeds the free tier's 10,000 workflow runs
Removing Novu branding requires the paid Pro plan or higher
Enterprise features (self-hosting, HIPAA BAA, dedicated SSO) require custom Enterprise pricing
